business_naming_philosophy: Specificity over genericism. Not 'Blacksmith' but a name that implies a person, a history, a specialty. Names suggest age, personality, regional character. No lame or repeating names. Every business is someone's livelihood.

businesses: Osric's Handles and Hafts (tool handles, weapon hafts)
The Cold Cellar (food storage, for hire)
The Evening Meal (meal service only, no lodging)
The Boiled Bone (poor man's soup kitchen)
The Straw Bed (floor space, shared, cheapest)
The Narrow Room (budget, private space barely)
Gorvan's Livery and Rest (horse care + rider lodging)
The Aldric Arms (upper mid-tier, proper meals)
The Merchant's Rest (upper tier, private rooms, secure storage)
The Factor's House (best in town, expensive, merchant clientele)
The Soldier's Welcome (cheap but tolerant of rough company)
The Tanner's Row (district, not single business)
The Dust Market (general goods — anything, not specialized)
The Sealed Ledger (notary, contracts, legal records)
The Assay House (metal valuation, official weights)
The Factor's Office (local representative of distant merchant houses)
The Open Hand (pawnbroker — loans against goods)
The Caravan Master (organizes and leads trade caravans)
The Manifest Office (harbor paperwork and cargo declarations)
The Barber of Durn Street (haircuts, tooth-pulling, minor surgery, bleeding)
The Letter Writer (scribing for the illiterate)
The Muck Cart (waste removal — essential, unglamorous)
The Night Soil Man (specifically sewage — different from general muck)
The Midwife (Kela Aldrath — three generations in this town)
The Rat Catcher (Soren Keldwick — also dogs, pest removal)
The Well Tender (maintenance of communal water supply)
The Lamplighter (maintains street lighting in wealthier districts)
The Messenger (Aldren's Post — paid courier service)
The Porter (Gorvan's Heavy Work — moving things)
The Grave Digger (Torven — also handles burial arrangements)
The Printer (rare, expensive — woodblock print, not press)
The Scribe (copying documents, not a notary, just labor)
The Surgeon (Halvard Croft — military trained, rough but competent)
The Apothecary (Brena Aldeth — extensive herb knowledge, cautious)
The Tooth-Puller (The Barber doubles — or dedicated specialist in larger towns)
The Herb Garden (Morra's Medicinals — raw plant materials, not compounds)
The Bone Setter (Keld Morrend — fractures, dislocations, doesn't do much else)
The Leech House (budget medical — bloodletting, minor treatment)
The Lazaret (isolation facility for contagious illness — town-funded, no profit)
The Temple of the Undying Ember (Durnward)
The Station of the Moor Communion
The Furrow Hall (Reclaimed Path — also community granary)
The Salt Silence Meeting Room (unmarked, unremarkable building)
The Pilgrim House (faith-subsidized lodging for travelers)
The Poorbox Office (distribution of charitable goods)
The Glassblower (Aldric Sorn — extremely rare outside cities)
The Bookbinder (Esven Keld — also repairs old books, literacy required to use fully)
The Chandler (Aldeth's Candles — also lamp oil and wicks)
The Dung Seller (Gorvan's Amendments — composted manure for farmers)
The Ice House (seasonal — stored winter ice for summer use, wealthy clients only)
The Perfumer (Sova's Scents — cities only, luxury trade)
The Cartographer (Brenmar's Maps — rare, expensive, questionable accuracy outside local area)
The Fencer (The unmarked shop — stolen goods, no name on door)
The Poisoner (no sign, no name, you're referred)
The Locksmith (Keld Mechanism — also makes the locks, charges to open them)

major_factions.name: The Grey Contracts
major_factions.current_contract: Border patrol for Lord-Marshal Brennwatch (Ironmark Borderlands)
major_factions.captain: Vael Sorn — keeps contracts to the letter and not a word further
major_factions.joining: Open recruitment when understaffed. Background check is: can you hold a weapon and follow an order

major_factions.name: The Bent Loom Guild
major_factions.type: Textile and general goods trading consortium
major_factions.actual_stance: Backing nephew Pelvar financially in Thornsveld succession
major_factions.joining: Merchant background or demonstrated commercial competence. They hire factors, clerks, caravan guards, warehouse staff.

major_factions.name: The Halverness Factors
major_factions.presence: Major offices in Keth and Amber Coast. Regional presence across Cauldenmoor.
major_factions.reputation: Politically neutral, commercially ruthless, reliable on contracts
major_factions.joining: Formal application. Literacy strongly preferred. References from existing members.

major_factions.name: The Hollow Road
major_factions.base: No fixed base — operates through no-man's-land settlements primarily
services: Unofficial banking for those who can't use official services
Moving people who need to move quietly
major_factions.joining: You don't apply. You make yourself useful to someone who's already in it.

major_factions.name: The Salvage Authority
major_factions.function: Licenses ruin excavation, taxes extraction, maintains road-guard force
major_factions.joining: Road-guard: open hiring, physical requirements. Licensing: fees and paperwork.

major_factions.name: The First Scholars / Archive of Keth
major_factions.function: Historical preservation, scholarship, Reckoning faith practice
major_factions.joining: Literacy required. Academic aptitude. They prefer to approach candidates rather than be approached.

major_factions.name: The Salt Silence
major_factions.base: Unknown. Present in every major port.
major_factions.function: Information brokering. Observation. Unclear ultimate purpose.
major_factions.joining: Unknown. You notice them watching you, or you don't.

major_factions.name: The Dust Council
major_factions.function: Governing body of Keth — seven seats, wealthiest trading houses
major_factions.joining: Become one of the seven wealthiest trading houses in Keth. Long-term goal only.

major_factions.name: The Drift Lords
major_factions.function: Pastoral economy, plateau control in practice
major_factions.joining: Acceptance by a Drift Lord clan. Requires demonstrated competence in plateau survival and earning of trust over time.

major_factions.name: The Corsair Throne
major_factions.function: Controls sea lanes, imposes unofficial tariffs, seeks formal political recognition
major_factions.joining: The Corsair Throne's quartermaster office is the entry point. No background questions asked.

major_factions.name: The Fen Lords
major_factions.base: The Free Fens (meets at Mossward twice yearly)
major_factions.function: Collective decision-making for Free Fens when external pressure requires it
major_factions.joining: Become the most powerful village head in your settlement. Earn recognition from the existing Lords.
